channel.title: Nature Materials
channel.description: Nature Materials is a multidisciplinary journal aimed at bringing together cutting-edge research across the entire spectrum of materials science and technology. Nature Materials covers all applied and fundamental aspects of the synthesis/processing, structure/composition, properties and performance of materials. Nature Materials provides a forum for the development of a common identity among materials scientists while encouraging researchers to cross established subdisciplinary lines. To achieve this, Nature Materials takes an interdisciplinary, integrated and balanced approach to all areas of materials research while fostering the exchange of ideas between scientists involved in different communities.
channel.dc:rights: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2026 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Leveraging the power of metasurfaces
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 23 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02469-3

Metasurfaces and metamaterials (or meta-optics) and their optoelectronic hybrid integration are set to drive the next era of computing, sensing and communications. As electronic systems reach fundamental limits, optical-enabled artificial intelligence emerges as a way forward, with meta-optics enabling speed, efficiency and scalability. We argue the need for comprehensive national innovation strategies to exploit the value of meta-optics in the photonics industry.
item.dc:title: Leveraging the power of metasurfaces
item.dc:creator: Marco Abbarchi
David Grosso
Badre Kerzabi
George Palikaras

item.title: Higgs mode-driven phase transitions in two-dimensional perovskites
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 20 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02472-8

A study of phase transitions in two-dimensional halide perovskites uncovers distinct thermal and optical pathways through Ginzburg–Landau theory. The work identifies an optically driven metastable tetragonal phase, excited by Higgs phonon modes, offering insights into symmetry-breaking dynamics.
item.dc:title: Higgs mode-driven phase transitions in two-dimensional perovskites
item.dc:creator: Gregory D. Scholes

item.title: Mechanical behaviour of additively manufactured metals
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02459-5

The non-equilibrium processing conditions inherent to additive manufacturing produce metals with unique microstructures and mechanical properties that are often unattainable through conventional routes. This Review provides an overview of distinctive mechanical behaviours and deformation mechanisms that arise from these characteristic microstructures, and discusses critical needs and emerging opportunities for alloy design and processing control to achieve exceptional mechanical properties in additively manufactured metals.
item.dc:title: Mechanical behaviour of additively manufactured metals
item.dc:creator: Ting Zhu
Wen Chen

item.title: Large-area non-stoichiometric phase transition in transition metal chalcogenide films
item.content:encoded:
Nature Materials, Published online: 16 January 2026; doi:10.1038/s41563-025-02471-9

Controllable and scalable phase transition of transition metal chalcogenides is challenging. Using in situ microscopic analysis, a non-stoichiometric phase transition from PdTe2 to PdTe is observed on the atomic scale, providing mechanistic insights into the scalable phase engineering of transition metal chalcogenide films and heterostructures.
item.dc:title: Large-area non-stoichiometric phase transition in transition metal chalcogenide films
item.dc:creator: Zhongqiang Chen
Jin-an Shi
Jianqi Huang
